title: MCP Management Hardening — Validation, Error Handling, Race Guards, Error Boundaries
consumer_purpose: Eliminate silent SDK failures, race conditions, unvalidated saves, and app-teardown ErrorBoundary escalation in the MCP server management UI
claim_atoms: transport validation guards command.length>0 and URL.parse before save
toggle/disconnectMutation wrapped in try/catch with toast on error
handleSave awaits async onSave instead of fire-and-forget
IPC get/set mcp servers wrapped in try/catch
per-server Semaphore serializes connect/disconnect on shared State
watch() defs assignment serialized through same per-server lock
ErrorBoundary wraps MCP dialog to prevent app-level teardown
content:
# MCP Management Hardening Plan ## Root Causes 1. **MCP-H-003/H-001**: JSON validation too lenient — empty command passes Array.isArray, timeout=0 passes, no URL parseability check 2. **MCP-H-005/H-006/H-013**: Mutation functions lack error boundaries — SDK failures are silent 3. **MCP-H-007**: handleSave fire-and-forget — parent async onSave is discarded 4. **MCP-H-008**: IPC handlers lack error wrapping — store access errors crash the renderer 5. **MCP-H-009/H-010/H-011**: No mutual exclusion on shared State — createAndStore/disconnect/watch race ## Fixes (ordered by surgical precision) ### Fix A: Validation guards (dialog-edit-mcp.tsx + ipc.ts) **Files**: `packages/app/src/components/dialog-edit-mcp.tsx`, `packages/desktop/src/main/ipc.ts`, `packages/app/src/components/dialog-select-mcp.tsx` **Changes**: 1. `dialog-edit-mcp.tsx handleSave()`: Add `const cmdStr = command().trim()` guard — if type is 'local' and cmdStr is empty or cmdStr.split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean) is empty, don't save. For remote type, add `URL.canParse(url())` guard before constructing entry. 2. `ipc.ts isValidMcpEntry()`: Change `if (config.type === "local" && !Array.isArray(config.command)) return false` to `if (config.type === "local" && (!Array.isArray(config.command) || config.command.length === 0)) return false`. Add `typeof config.timeout !== "number" || config.timeout >= 0` — Number('')=0 is valid, the real guard is at the form level. 3. `dialog-select-mcp.tsx submitAdd()`: Add `URL.canParse(url)` check before calling addMutation. ### Fix B: Mutation error handling (dialog-select-mcp.tsx) **File**: `packages/app/src/components/dialog-select-mcp.tsx` **Changes**: 1. Add import: `import { showToast } from "@opencode-ai/ui/toast"` (not currently imported) 2. `toggle.mutationFn`: Wrap entire body in try/catch. On catch, call `showToast({ title: language.t("dialog.mcp.error"), description: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err), variant: "error" })` and throw to propagate to onError. 3. `toggle`: Add `onError` handler matching the same showToast pattern (belt-and-suspenders with try/catch) 4. `disconnectMutation.mutationFn`: Same try/catch with showToast pattern 5. `disconnectMutation`: Add onError handler with showToast ### Fix C: handleSave async await (dialog-edit-mcp.tsx) **File**: `packages/app/src/components/dialog-edit-mcp.tsx` **Changes**: 1. Change `const handleSave = () =>` to `const handleSave = async () =>` 2. Change `props.onSave(entry)` to `await props.onSave(entry)` inside handleSave 3. Update `Props.onSave` type from `(entry: McpServerEntry) => void` to `(entry: McpServerEntry) => Promise` for type correctness ### Fix D: IPC error wrapping (ipc.ts via produce_fragment) **File**: `packages/desktop/src/main/ipc.ts` **Changes** (use `produce_fragment` due to Lane 1 contention): 1. `get-desktop-mcp-servers`: Wrap body in try/catch, `console.error` and return `[]` on error 2. `set-desktop-mcp-servers`: Wrap body in try/catch, `console.error` on error ### Fix E: Per-server Semaphore (mcp/index.ts) **File**: `packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts` (already dirty — TuiEvent inline stub) **Changes**: 1. Add `Semaphore` to existing `import { Effect, Exit, Layer, Option, Context, Schema, Stream } from "effect"` — now `import { Effect, Exit, Layer, Option, Context, Schema, Stream, Semaphore } from "effect"` 2. In the layer generator, add `const serverLocks = new Map()` 3. Add `withServerLock` helper following `file-lock.ts` pattern: ```ts const withServerLock = (name: string) => { let sem = serverLocks.get(name) if (!sem) { sem = Semaphore.makeUnsafe(1) serverLocks.set(name, sem) } return sem } ``` 4. Wrap `createAndStore` body: `yield* withServerLock(name).withPermits(1)(Effect.gen(function* () { /* original body */ }))` 5. Wrap `disconnect` body: `yield* withServerLock(name).withPermits(1)(Effect.gen(function* () { /* original body */ }))` 6. Wrap `watch()` handler body through `bridge.promise(withServerLock(name).withPermits(1)(Effect.gen(function* () { /* original handler */ })))` — this serializes the TOCTOU defs assignment through the same lock ### Fix F: ErrorBoundary around MCP dialog (use-session-commands.tsx) **File**: `packages/app/src/pages/session/use-session-commands.tsx` **Changes**: 1. Add `ErrorBoundary` to the existing `import { ..., ErrorBoundary } from "solid-js"` (currently imports `Show` only — extend the import) 2. In `chooseMcp`, wrap ``: ```tsx dialog.show(() => ( { console.error("MCP dialog error:", err) return
MCP dialog error. Close and try again.
}}> )) ``` ## Impact Assessment | File | Before | After | Risk | |---|---|---|---| | dialog-edit-mcp.tsx | handleSave sync, no validation | async, validates command/URL | Low — parent onSave already async | | dialog-select-mcp.tsx | silent SDK failures | toast on error, URL validation | Low — adds error visibility | | ipc.ts (via fragment) | store access throws to caller | try/catch returns [] | Low — better failure mode | | mcp/index.ts (dirty) | shared state races | per-server semaphore serialize | Medium — deadlock if lock leaked but operations bounded | | use-session-commands.tsx | MCP error tears down app | ErrorBoundary catches locally | Low — follows app.tsx pattern | ## Risks 1. **Fix E**: Effect.withPermits(1) acquires a permit; if the wrapped effect never completes (hanging network connect), the semaphore blocks for that server. Mitigation: connect has timeout via `connectTransport`'s timeout parameter. Disconnect is synchronous. 2. **Fix E**: `Semaphore.makeUnsafe(1)` is effect-unsafe creation (doesn't require runtime). This matches the existing `file-lock.ts` pattern in the codebase. Safe because locks are created inside the layer effect. 3. **Fix C**: SolidJS Button `onClick` returns void. An async function returns Promise — TypeScript assigns Promise to void in event handlers. 4. **Fix A**: `URL.canParse` may reject valid MCP URLs with unusual formats. Test for typical formats (http://, https://, ws://). 5. **Fix B**: Adding `showToast` requires the toast module to be available in the renderer context. It's already used in `use-session-commands.tsx` so the module is loaded. ## Validation Strategy 1. `bun typecheck` in packages/app and packages/opencode — no new type errors 2. `bun test` in packages/app — existing tests pass (focus on MCP dialog tests) 3. Manual: Open MCP dialog → Add server with empty URL → should show validation error 4. Manual: Rapid toggle (click switch on/off quickly) → each connect/disconnect serialized, no duplicate clients in state 5. Manual: Kill MCP process during toggle → toast shows error, app stays stable 6. Manual: Edit server → save → verify store contains valid entry (empty command rejected) 7. Manual: Trigger render error in MCP dialog → ErrorBoundary catches, app shell survives ## Architectural Decisions 1. **Follow file-lock.ts pattern** for semaphore — same API (`Semaphore.makeUnsafe(1)`, `.withPermits(1)`), already proven in codebase 2. **Use produce_fragment for ipc.ts** — Lane 1 (Plugins) may be editing same file; fragment avoids merge conflicts 3. **ErrorBoundary from solid-js** — same import as app.tsx:171, no new dependency 4. **No refactoring** — all changes are additive guard clauses or wrappers, zero restructuring 5. **Dirty file preservation**: mcp/index.ts has uncommitted TuiEvent stub — our changes are additive (add Semaphore import + lock map), do not touch the TuiEvent region
revision_notes: Corrected showToast import status (not pre-existing, must be added); added dirty-file preservation note; expanded validation guards for handleSave; added exact before/after code patterns for each fix
